content:
--sidechaining your bass very lightly to your snares. i do the same with my hats & pads.

--lets say you have a snare with a 2second tail and youve got a 3 second tail on your vocals. try and separate them on the frequency spectrum a bit. an easy way of doing that is by eq'ing your reverbs on return tracks.

--instead of slamming the kick against a limiter until its just a square wave of noise, try layering with samples that utilize the whole frequency spectrum. widen up your top layer. and compress in to taste


Talking Heads
separate individual statements/ideas, unshared/kept private, on what each person wants the song to say -- withdraw/separate, come up with a few bars separately -- come together, listen -- work one into the other

https://makingmusic.ableton.com/presets-as-starting-points
